requirements.problem: Restaurant owners spend too much time answering repeated customer questions across scattered channels.
requirements.target_user: Small restaurant owners and managers who handle customer questions manually.
pain_points: Repeated questions about opening hours, menu items, allergies, reservations, and delivery.
Customer communication is scattered across Instagram, phone calls, Google reviews, and Facebook.
Owners risk missing requests or giving outdated information.
Manual replies take time away from operations and customer service.
risks: Allergy or dietary answers may be risky if menu information is incomplete.
Outdated menu, delivery, or reservation information could mislead customers.
Fully automated replies should not be enabled before owner review.
assumptions: The founder has at least a few customer conversations or notes.
The first version should be reviewed by a human before customer-facing use.
The MVP should avoid complex integrations until the core workflow is validated.

mvp_plan.recommended_mvp: A human-reviewed AI FAQ assistant for restaurant owners.
in_scope: Generate editable FAQ answers from the restaurant information.
Answer sample customer questions in a test console.
Flag risky allergy, dietary, or missing-information questions for owner review.
Create evaluation test cases for common and risky questions.
out_of_scope: Live Instagram, Facebook, POS, or delivery integrations.
Fully automated customer replies without owner approval.
Payments, reservations engine, or full CRM features.
success_criteria: Owner can enter restaurant information and receive clear FAQ drafts.
Assistant handles common questions with useful answers.
Risky allergy or missing-information cases are flagged instead of guessed.
The workflow produces actionable Jira tickets and evaluation results.

components.name: Founder Notes Input
components.purpose: Collect messy customer notes, discovery findings, or business context.

components.name: Requirement Extraction
components.purpose: Identify problem, user, pain points, risks, and assumptions.

components.name: MVP Scope Engine
components.purpose: Separate in-scope MVP work from later-stage product ideas.

components.name: Architecture Planner
components.purpose: Create a simple technical structure before implementation.

components.name: Jira Ticket Generator
components.purpose: Convert scope into user stories, acceptance criteria, and definitions of done.

components.name: Evaluation Layer
components.purpose: Score content quality, domain fit, and system reliability.

components.name: Final Summary Generator
components.purpose: Create a founder-ready delivery summary and next-step plan.

data_flow: MVP scope is defined with clear out-of-scope boundaries.
Architecture and Jira tickets are generated.
Outputs are evaluated using content, domain, and reliability metrics.
Final summary is produced for founders, mentors, or team members.
next_production_layers: Replace deterministic modules with Claude/Codex prompt calls where useful.
Connect Jira through MCP or API after human approval gates are defined.
Connect GitHub workflow through GitHub API/MCP and PR templates.
Add database storage, authentication, and prompt regression tests for production.

jira_tickets.user_story: As a founder, I want to generate mvp analysis output so that I can move from messy feedback to structured MVP execution.

acceptance_criteria: At least five tickets are created
Each ticket has definition of done

jira_tickets.user_story: As a founder, I want to add evaluation metrics so that I can move from messy feedback to structured MVP execution.

jira_tickets.user_story: As a founder, I want to create final founder summary so that I can move from messy feedback to structured MVP execution.
jira_tickets.description: Generate a concise delivery summary that can be shared with team members, mentors, or investors.
acceptance_criteria: Limitations and next steps are included
definition_of_done: Relevant tests or evaluation checks pass

content_quality.name: Problem Accuracy
content_quality.notes: The main operational problem is clearly identified.

content_quality.name: Target User Accuracy
content_quality.notes: The target user is specific and founder-friendly.

content_quality.name: Pain Point Coverage
content_quality.notes: Major repeated-question, scattered-channel, and time-waste pains are captured.

content_quality.name: MVP Scope Quality
content_quality.notes: The MVP is small, useful, and avoids full CRM/POS overbuild.

content_quality.name: Jira Ticket Actionability
content_quality.notes: Tickets include user stories, acceptance criteria, priority, and branch guidance.

content_quality.name: Final Summary Quality
content_quality.notes: Summary is clear enough for a mentor, team member, or founder update.

domain_fit.name: Domain Relevance
domain_fit.notes: The workflow fits small restaurant owner operations.

domain_fit.name: Allergy/Menu Risk Handling
domain_fit.notes: Allergy and missing menu information are flagged for owner review.

domain_fit.name: Missing Information Handling
domain_fit.notes: The design avoids guessing when details are unavailable.

domain_fit.name: Human Review Fit
domain_fit.notes: Owner approval is required before risky customer-facing use.

domain_fit.name: Practical Usefulness
domain_fit.notes: The MVP would help reduce repeated manual responses.

system_reliability.name: Required Field Completeness
system_reliability.notes: Core sections are generated: requirements, scope, architecture, tickets, evaluation, summary.

system_reliability.name: JSON/API Structure
system_reliability.notes: The API uses typed Pydantic models and predictable output structure.

system_reliability.name: Test Pass Readiness
system_reliability.notes: The reference workflow is covered by pytest checks.

system_reliability.name: Edge Case Handling
system_reliability.notes: Short notes and regulated-domain cues are handled with assumptions/risks.

system_reliability.name: Production Readiness
system_reliability.notes: This is a workshop reference system; production needs live LLM, Jira/GitHub, auth, storage, and monitoring.

evaluation.recommendation: Workshop-ready. Use as a deterministic reference system, then add live Claude/Codex, Jira, GitHub, and MCP integrations as the advanced layer.

final_summary:
# MVPFlow AI Delivery Summary

## Problem
Restaurant owners spend too much time answering repeated customer questions across scattered channels.

## Target User
Small restaurant owners and managers who handle customer questions manually.

## Recommended MVP
A human-reviewed AI FAQ assistant for restaurant owners.

## MVP Scope
- Paste or upload restaurant information.
- Generate editable FAQ answers from the restaurant information.
- Answer sample customer questions in a test console.
- Flag risky allergy, dietary, or missing-information questions for owner review.
- Create evaluation test cases for common and risky questions.

## Key Risks
- Allergy or dietary answers may be risky if menu information is incomplete.
- Outdated menu, delivery, or reservation information could mislead customers.
- Fully automated replies should not be enabled before owner review.

## Architecture Pattern
Human-in-the-loop AI workflow MVP

## Jira Backlog
- MVP-001: Define restaurant FAQ MVP scope
- MVP-002: Build founder notes input workflow
- MVP-003: Generate MVP analysis output
- MVP-004: Generate Jira-style execution tickets
- MVP-005: Add evaluation metrics
- MVP-006: Create final founder summary

## Evaluation Result
Overall score: 4.66/5

Recommendation: Workshop-ready. Use as a deterministic reference system, then add live Claude/Codex, Jira, GitHub, and MCP integrations as the advanced layer.

## Next Steps
1. Review generated tickets with the founder/team.
2. Select the first implementation ticket.
3. Build through a GitHub branch and PR.
4. Run tests and domain-specific evaluation.
5. Update the final summary after each milestone.
